title: 10 best herbal creams for tinnitus

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: May improve blood flow to the inner ear, which could help reduce tinnitus symptoms by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the affected areas.
St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum): Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus.
Bacopa monnieri: May have neuroprotective effects, helping to preserve hearing-related neural pathways and potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with tinnitus because of its potential to improve blood flow to the ears. Tinnitus is often associated with reduced blood flow and oxidative stress, which can lead to ringing or buzzing sounds. Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ears. Additionally, its ability to increase oxygen delivery to the ears may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may provide relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Biflavonoids, particularly Ginkgo Flavone Glycosides): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory nerve, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These sesquiterpenes have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative damage in the auditory nerve and cochlea, contributing to tinnitus relief.

Vasodilatory Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These compounds may help improve blood flow to the inner ear, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including tinnitus, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba is also used, as it is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 leaves of ginkgo biloba from mature trees in late summer to early fall.
Dry the leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar.
Mix the powder with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly over low heat until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120°f.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Betony's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms by calming the auditory system and reducing pressure on the ears.

Antioxidant property: Betony's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cells in the cochlea, leading to tinnitus and hearing loss, thereby protecting the auditory system from further harm.

Nerve-calming property: Betony's natural compounds, such as triterpenoids, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help calm the overactive nerves that often accompany tinnitus, reducing ringing or buzzing sensations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stachys officinalis, also known as betony, creams helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains flavonoids and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the ear and promote healing. Betony cream may also improve blood flow to the ear, helping to reduce noise-induced damage and alleviate symptoms of tinnitus. Additionally, its soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate tinnitus. By addressing these underlying causes, betony cream may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system, which can contribute to tinnitus development.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid, may help mitigate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, as well as improving blood flow to the ears.

Betalains: Betalains, a type of antioxidant, may help reduce oxidative stress in the auditory system, which can contribute to tinnitus development, and may also have anti-inflammatory effects to alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe ear inflammation and reduce tinnitus symptoms.
Barks: Utilized for their astringent properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear area, helping to alleviate tinnitus.
(Although not commonly used)  Stems: May be used due to the presence of some bioactive compounds with potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh stachys officinalis leaves in the early morning and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep the leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes then let the mixture cool down to room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of glycerin.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler until they melt completely in 10 minutes.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined in 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear associated with tinnitus.

Hypotensive activity: St John's Wort creams containing hyperforin and other flavonoids can help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and flow to the ears, which may alleviate symptoms of tinnitus caused by vascular issues.

Anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the ear associated with tinnitus, providing relief from symptoms like ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with tinnitus because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, contributing to the development of tinnitus. By alleviating these underlying factors, Hypericum perforatum creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds, providing relief for individuals affected by this condition. Some studies suggest its potential in reducing tinnitus severity.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system.

N-Acylated flavonoids (e.g., hyperoside, quercetin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the auditory system from damage and reduce tinnitus symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Biflavonoids (e.g., amentoflavone): Biflavonoids, such as amentoflavone, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce tinnitus symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their potential therapeutic effects.
Flowers: The flowers contain hyperforin and hypericin, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for tinnitus treatment.
Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum are also rich in flavonoids and may be used to enhance the efficacy of creams for tinnitus relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them in a single layer on a baking sheet for 1 week.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of beeswax and 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and let it cool before transferring it to a tin container.

remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: Oat cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant and free radical-scavenging activity: The antioxidants present in oat cream can help neutralize free radicals that may be damaging the hair cells in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Moisturizing and protective properties: Oat cream's moisturizing effects can help soothe dry skin and mucous membranes in the ear canal, reducing irritation and inflammation that may exacerbate tinnitus.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, creams helps with tinnitus because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains saponins, which have been shown to reduce stress and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, Avena sativa creams may help to improve blood flow to the ears, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By reducing stress and inflammation, Avena sativa creams may provide relief from the ringing and buzzing associated with tinnitus, offering a natural and gentle solution for sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: Avenanthramides, a class of phenolic compounds found in Avena sativa, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, contributing to the alleviation of tinnitus symptoms.

Fatty acids: The presence of fatty acids, particularly oleic and linoleic acids, in Avena sativa creams may help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the ears, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Silicic acid: Silicic acid, a naturally occurring compound found in Avena sativa, may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, contributing to the alleviation of tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are used to make creams for tinnitus due to their high content of antioxidants and fatty acids that help soothe and calm the auditory system.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are used to make creams for tinnitus due to their high content of saponins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ears.

Stems: The stems of Avena sativa are used to make creams for tinnitus due to their high content of mucilages that help soothe and calm the auditory system, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried avena sativa roots from a reputable supplier or local herb store.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered avena sativa with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil melt.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool before use.

remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian cream's sedative and anxiolytic properties can help reduce anxiety, stress, and tension, which are common underlying causes of tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate inflammation in the inner ear, which is thought to contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant effects: Valerian cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate tinnitus.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with tinnitus because of its ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The antispasmodic and anti-anxiety properties of valerian root have been found to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for tinnitus episodes. By soothing the nervous system and promoting a state of calm, valerian creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing or buzzing in the ears. This natural remedy offers a potential non-invasive solution for those seeking relief from tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-induced tinnitus.
Isovaleric acid amide: Isovaleric acid amide has a sedative effect, which may help alleviate the stress and anxiety that exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Valerenal: Valerenal has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, potentially reducing the emotional impact of tinnitus and promoting relaxation and sleep, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in valerenic acid, a compound with sedative and analgesic properties that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: They contain valeranone, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system, potentially helping to reduce tinnitus.
Barks: They are rich in valerenic acid and other compounds with sedative and analgesic properties, which may help to calm the nervous system and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Steep 250 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The calming compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid and citronellal, have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral properties may help combat viral infections that can contribute to tinnitus, such as herpes simplex virus, by inhibiting the replication of viruses in the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including rosmarinic acid and essential oils. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system. By calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis creams can also help to reduce the severity and frequency of tinnitus symptoms, providing relief and improving overall hearing health. This natural remedy has shown promise in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Linalool: As a terpene, Linalool has sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related tinnitus.
Melissic Acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a protective effect on the auditory system, potentially reducing noise-induced hearing loss and tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are used because they contain melissic acid, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are used because they are rich in volatile oils, particularly linalool, which is thought to have anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects that may help soothe tinnitus.

Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are used because they contain a higher concentration of melissic acid and other active compounds compared to other parts, which may contribute to their potential therapeutic benefits for tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Infuse 50g of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:10.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal infused oil.
Mix the herbal infused oil with beeswax and distilled water in a ratio of 50:25:25 to create a cream base.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, thereby alleviating the symptoms.
Antioxidant Properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of tinnitus.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal and surrounding tissues, which may be a contributing factor to tinnitus.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with tinnitus because of its soothing and calming properties. The active compounds in lavender oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, lavender creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, allowing individuals to find relief from the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with the condition. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for tinnitus sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with tinnitus by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for the condition, and promoting relaxation through its sedative effects.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to the reduction of tinnitus symptoms by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate the underlying inflammation associated with the condition.
Lavandulol: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: Contain calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and reduce tinnitus-related stress.
Seeds: May contain compounds that help regulate blood flow and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day after morning dew has evaporated.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the dried flowers in 1 cup of hot water for 10 minutes at 212°f.
Strain the liquid and combine it with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes at 140°f to melt the beeswax and butter.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens and cools down to room temperature.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hops contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in hops cream may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve, alleviating tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Hops contain sedative and anxiolytic compounds that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for tinnitus.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus, also known as hops, creams helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in hops, such as alpha-bisabolol and lupulone, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus. Additionally, hops have been found to have a neuroprotective effect, which can help to reduce the perception of tinnitus. This makes hops creams a promising natural remedy for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Lupulone: A sesquiterpene with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the auditory system and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Humulone: A sesquiterpene with potential neuroprotective and antioxidant effects, which may help protect the auditory nerve and reduce oxidative stress associated with tinnitus.

α-Humulene: A sesquiterpene with potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the auditory system, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly beta-caryophyllene, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating tinnitus.
Seeds: They are used because they contain flavonoids and lignans, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: They are used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the relief of tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 ounce of dried humulus lupulus flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to collect the liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has cooled slightly.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile cream's antioxidants can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate tinnitus symptoms and contribute to hearing loss.

Anxiolytic property: Chamomile cream's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related tinnitus symptoms, as many people experience anxiety or tension that amplifies their tinnitus sounds.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with tinnitus because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the auditory nerve, which can contribute to tinnitus. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for tinnitus episodes. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile creams may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound helps alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, which can contribute to the condition.
Luteolin: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of tinnitus by protecting the auditory system from damage.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to tinnitus, as well as promote relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help to calm the auditory system and reduce tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate tinnitus.

Seeds (Note: While Matricaria chamomilla is primarily known for its flowers, the seeds are sometimes used in herbal remedies and could be used in some tinnitus creams. However, the seeds are not as commonly used as the flowers or leaves for this purpose): Their oil, rich in sesquiterpenes, can have anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Infuse 2 cups of carrier oil with the dried flowers for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 1/4 cup of the herbal extract with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to form a smooth and creamy herbal matricaria chamomilla cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream, like caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage cells in the ear and contribute to tinnitus, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Glycemic regulatory properties: Kansas coneflower's glycosides help regulate blood sugar levels, which can be beneficial for individuals with tinnitus as high blood sugar has been linked to increased oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling in the ear canal and surrounding tissues. The cream may also help to soothe and calm the auditory nerve, reducing the perception of sound. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, which may help to combat underlying infections that contribute to tinnitus. Its natural antiseptic properties may also aid in promoting ear health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation that may contribute to tinnitus.
Chlorogenic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ear from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used due to their high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain a significant amount of alkaloids and other compounds that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: The roots are also used again, as they are rich in inulin and other bioactive compounds that may have a beneficial effect on tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in the early morning to preserve their potency.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried roots in 4 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make the infusion.
Strain and measure 1/2 cup of the infusion to mix with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes to 1 hour before applying to the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort creams helps with tinnitus because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that target the root causes of the condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as scopoletin and figwort glycosides, reduce inflammation in the ear canal and auditory nerve, which can help alleviate ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing stress and discomfort often linked to tinnitus. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal figwort creams can provide effective relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Figwort cream's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms by soothing damaged auditory cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory agents in figwort cream can help reduce swelling and irritation in the inner ear, thereby alleviating tinnitus-related discomfort and restoring normal hearing function.

Vasodilatory properties: Figwort's vasodilatory compounds can help dilate blood vessels in the ear, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the affected areas, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by reducing stress and promoting healthy ear function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting ear health.
Barks: Contain salannin, a compound that has been shown to reduce hearing loss and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scrophularia nodosa leaves and flowers weighing approximately 500g for drying.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried scrophularia nodosa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered scrophularia nodosa with two parts of coconut oil and beeswax, heating gently until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars and store in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before use, allowing the cream to mature and thicken naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian creams helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to soothe and calm the auditory system. The gentle, non-invasive cream is absorbed through the skin, allowing its active ingredients to reach the affected areas deep within the ear. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, yellow gentian creams help alleviate tinnitus symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds. As a result, individuals experiencing tinnitus may find significant relief from their condition with regular use of these natural remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gentian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the ear that can contribute to tinnitus.
Antioxidant: Gentian's antioxidant properties may help protect the ears from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Anxiolytic: Gentian has been traditionally used as a calming agent and may help alleviate anxiety and stress-related tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems store valuable nutrients and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the auditory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality gentiana lutea roots and dry them in the shade for 7-10 days.
Grind dried gentiana lutea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered gentiana lutea with 40 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of olive oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, until all ingredients are fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized jars, allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature, then store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with tinnitus because its essential oils possess a unique combination of properties that can alleviate ringing in the ears. The cream's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects help to relax the ear muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, lemon verbena's sedative properties can calm the nervous system, which may be contributing to tinnitus symptoms. By applying the cream directly to the ear canal or behind the ear, individuals can potentially experience relief from their tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon verbena creams may help to alleviate the chronic inflammation that is often associated with tinnitus, reducing symptoms such as ear pressure, ringing, and buzzing.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon verbena's calming effects can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which are common comorbidities with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying emotional states, lemon verbena creams may provide additional relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the ear.
Barks: Contains bioactive compounds like limonene and citral, which have been shown to reduce ringing noise perception and improve tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven for exactly 30 minutes at 150°f to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine one tablespoon of dried verbena officinalis with two tablespoons of sweet almond oil and four drops of lavender essential oil.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours under low heat, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with two ounces of beeswax pellets, one teaspoon of coconut oil, and a pinch of vitamin e oil to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate the discomfort and irritation associated with this condition. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can help to calm the ringing or buzzing sensations caused by tinnitus. Additionally, pot marigold creams may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, further contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits for individuals with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the key active compound in pot marigold, has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear canal, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing discomfort and promoting healthy ear function.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development or worsening.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula's ability to accelerate wound healing can help repair any underlying ear injuries or infections that may be contributing to tinnitus, promoting a healthy environment for ear function and reducing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an ideal source for creating medicinal creams that can effectively alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are essential in soothing ear irritation and reducing noise-induced hearing loss associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil with 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl and melt in the microwave for 30 seconds at 30-second intervals.
Combine the ground calendula powder with the melted oil and wax mixture, stirring well to ensure thorough incorporation for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and place them in a warm water bath at 65°c for 15 minutes to set.
Allow the creams to cool completely and solidify before use within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with tinnitus because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce oxidative stress in the auditory system. The herbs in these creams, such as turmeric and ginger, have been shown to decrease inflammation in the ear canals and middle ear, which is often linked to tinnitus symptoms. By addressing underlying inflammation and promoting healthy ear function, herbal dandelion creams can provide natural relief from tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage ear tissues and contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Tonic and cleansing properties: Dandelion's gentle, non-irritating nature helps to tone and cleanse the ears, promoting healthy ear function and reducing the likelihood of tinnitus.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and dry them in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Grind 20g of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 50ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10ml of beeswax until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until melted.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly allowing it to cool completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and essential fatty acids. The cream's natural ingredients work together to soothe and calm the inner ear, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to tinnitus. Buds: Parsley buds are rich in essential oils and flavonoids that have been shown to have antihistaminic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and chop them into fine pieces weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the chopped petroselinum crispum leaves with 30 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for four hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 10 grams of beeswax, 5 milliliters of coconut oil, and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for approximately 20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley contains high levels of antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and flavonoids, which help protect the body from free radical damage and oxidative stress, both of which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley has anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which may help reduce inflammation in the auditory system that could be contributing to tinnitus symptoms.

Antihistaminic properties: Parsley contains histamine-reducing compounds like apigenin and quercetin, which may help alleviate histamine-related tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with tinnitus because they contain menthol, a natural ingredient that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the inner ear. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from organic sources weighing approximately 200 grams.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days.
Blend dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Infuse the blend under low heat for 30 minutes then strain and discard solids after cooling.
Mix the infused liquid with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at a temperature of 160°f for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation in the ear canal. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccant methods within 24 hours of collection to preserve active compounds.
Infuse 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves in 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth and mix with 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature (around 60°c) until the wax is fully incorporated and the cream thickens, stirring occasionally over 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with tinnitus because they contain flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear. remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula arvensis flowers at peak bloom in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or low-temperature drying equipment for 7 hours.
Grind 100g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder and store in an airtight container.
Melt 200g of beeswax in a double boiler while adding 20g of grated coconut oil and 10g of ground calendula powder stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before pouring into sterilized glass jars and allowing to solidify at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for tinnitus are topical remedies infused with natural herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus. These creams work by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, thereby soothing the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with the condition. Some of the best herbal creams for tinnitus include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Calendula officinalis, and Vitex agnus-castus. If left untreated, tinnitus can disrupt sleep, cause anxiety, and even lead to social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal creams are earaches, ear infections, and hearing loss. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and garlic have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal ear problems. Additionally, herbal creams used for conditions like otosclerosis and Meniere's disease also show promise in alleviating tinnitus-like symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with tinnitus is a blend of Ginkgo biloba, Aloe vera, and Calendula. Ginkgo biloba is known to improve blood flow to the ears, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Aloe vera soothes and calms the skin, while Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce ear discomfort. Using these creams together may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by promoting ear health and reducing inflammation. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with tinnitus because they contain compounds that improve blood circulation to the ears, reducing inflammation and damage to the delicate structures responsible for hearing. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba also protect the auditory system from oxidative stress, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus. By addressing these underlying causes, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, providing relief from the constant ringing or buzzing sounds that can disrupt daily life.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which contributes to the development and progression of the condition.

Terpenoids (specifically bilobalide): They help with tinnitus by protecting the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals and improving blood flow to the affected area, which can alleviate symptoms.

Ginkgo flavone glycosides (specifically quercetin and kaempferol): They help with tinnitus by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to the alleviation of tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used due to their high flavonoid content, which is believed to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in creams for tinnitus due to their rich antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the ears.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are also used in creams for tinnitus, possibly due to their high content of terpenoids, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves from healthy trees and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 4 cups of boiling water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl and discard solids.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Combine the infused liquid with 2 tablespoons of distilled water and 1 teaspoon of beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with tinnitus because they contain active compounds found in ginger, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may alleviate the underlying causes of tinnitus. remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams for tinnitus because of their high concentration of gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams for tinnitus because of their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of zingiber officinale root, 50g of coconut oil, 20g of beeswax, and 10ml of jojoba oil.
Grate the 100g of zingiber officinale root and infuse in 200ml of carrier oil at 45 minutes.
Melt 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler at 10 minutes.
Add the infused zingiber officinale mixture and 10ml of jojoba oil to the melted mixture at 5 minutes.
Whip the mixture at a high speed for 10 minutes until it thickens into a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with tinnitus because they contain the soothing properties of lemon balm, a natural herb known for its calming effects. remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves by hand in early morning to ensure highest essential oil content.
Dry melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Infuse melissa officinalis leaves in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the infused oil mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Mix the cooled infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 1:1:0.5 for cream base.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with tinnitus because they contain the extract of oat kernel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried avena sativa in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Blend the mixture with the beeswax and shea butter until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the inner ear and promote healing. Quercetin: A flavonoid with antioxidant properties, which helps to mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, potentially relieving tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers at peak potency and dry them for 2 weeks.
Combine 10 grams of dried flowers with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of jojoba oil to the mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with tinnitus because they are derived from the Passiflora incarnata plant, also known as passionflower, which has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other active compounds that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Infuse dried flower petals in 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 1:2 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt until smooth.
Add 30ml of the infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture and stir well.

remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has completed its life cycle.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place for 7 to 14 days to reduce moisture content to 12.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered roots with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove any impurities and pour into glass jars.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with tinnitus because of its unique properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and gently clean them in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water.
Infuse the flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil at 180-190°f for 10-15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vitex agnus-castus berries from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 100g of the dried berries with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax to the mixture and heat it until the waxes melt completely.
Pour the hot mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes before use.
